title: RemappingRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Remapping recipe. Combines one or more input images and produces a single categorical output (`class` band) by assigning each pixel to whichever target class's constraint expression it satisfies. The constraint expressions can reference any band of any input image, so this is a flexible 'map raster pixels to a new categorical scheme' tool — typical uses include reclassifying a land-cover map, deriving a categorical product from continuous inputs (e.g. forest/non-forest from canopy cover), or fusing multiple input layers into a single class label per pixel.

**Pipeline.** (1) Load each entry in `inputImagery.images` and select its `includedBands`. (2) For each `legend.entries[i]`, build a per-pixel mask from its `constraints` combined per `booleanOperator`. (3) Mosaic the entries (in REVERSED order — `ee.mosaic` lets EARLIER entries override later ones, but the recipe reverses first so LATER entries win in the source order). (4) Pixels matching no entry are masked out.

**AOI semantics.** No separate AOI field — the AOI is the geometry of the first input image (`inputImagery.images[0]`).

**`constraint.image` differs from indexChange.** Where the indexChange recipe's constraints use the literal string `'this-recipe'`, remapping's constraints reference an input image by its `imageId`. The mapping panel populates this from the user's image selection.

This schema describes the persisted `model` of an *initialized* recipe. Cross-field constraints that JSON Schema cannot express are enforced by the companion rules.

inputImagery.title: RemappingInputImagery
inputImagery.description: Source images that contribute features. At least one image is required. Constraints in `legend.entries[*].constraints` reference these images by their `imageId`.

inputImage.title: RemappingInputImage
inputImage.description: A single source image. The image's `imageId` is the join key referenced from `legend.entries[*].constraints[*].image`.

imageId.description: Stable id (UUID) for this input image. Constraints reference the image via this field.

type.description: What `id` refers to. `RECIPE_REF` — id of another SEPAL recipe whose image output is consumed. `ASSET` — Earth Engine Image / ImageCollection asset id.

id.description: SEPAL recipe id (when type=RECIPE_REF) or Earth Engine asset id (when type=ASSET).

bands.description: Per-band metadata, keyed by band name. For categorical bands carries `{values, labels, palette}`; for continuous bands typically empty. Populated by the GUI on image selection — used by the Mapping panel to know which bands have categorical legends and what the class values are. The LLM does not need to synthesize this; pull it from the loaded recipe/asset state.

visualizations.description: Optional visualization presets cached from the source. Cosmetic; the LLM should leave it absent.

includedBands.description: Bands selected to be available to the constraint expressions. Must contain every band that appears in any `legend.entries[*].constraints[*].band` referencing this image.

band.description: Band name in the source image.

type.description: Whether the band is categorical (limited integer values, used with `class` constraint operator) or continuous (numeric, used with `<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=`, `range` operators).

legendEntries.description: Legend of the source band (mirrors the source classification's classes). Only meaningful when `type='categorical'` — the Mapping panel uses these to populate the class-picker UI for `class` constraints. Empty (or absent) for continuous bands.

legend.title: RemappingLegend
legend.description: Target classes the recipe produces, each with the constraint expression used to assign pixels to that class.

id.description: UUID for stable React keys; also used as the path key when the Mapping panel updates the entry's constraints.

value.description: Output class value stored in the `class` band. Must be unique across entries.

color.description: Hex color (`#RRGGBB`) used when visualizing this class.

booleanOperator.description:
How `constraints` are combined.

- `'and'` — every constraint must be satisfied (the recipe reduces with `sum().eq(constraints.size())`).
- `'or'` — any constraint suffices (max of constraint masks).

Ignored when `constraints` has 0 or 1 entries. Required only when `constraints` has 2+ entries.

constraints.description: The constraint expression for this class. An empty array (or omitted) masks the entire image for this class — typically not what you want; it produces a class with no pixels.

constraint.title: RemappingConstraint
constraint.description:
A single comparison applied to a band of one of the input images. The shape varies by operator:

- `class` — categorical comparison: pixel matches if the band's value is in `selectedClasses`.
- `range` — pixel value lies between `from` and `to`, with `fromInclusive`/`toInclusive` flags.
- `<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=` — comparison against `value`.

Note the unicode `≤` / `≥` (U+2264 / U+2265) — these are the actual operator strings the backend expects, NOT `<=` / `>=`.

description.description: Human-readable summary shown in the GUI; not used by the backend.

image.description: The `imageId` of an entry in `inputImagery.images` whose band this constraint reads. NOT the literal string `'this-recipe'` (that's used by indexChange, not remapping).

band.description: Band name to read from the referenced input image. The band must appear in that image's `includedBands`.

operator.description: Comparison operator. `class` is for categorical bands; the rest are for continuous bands. `≤` and `≥` are unicode U+2264 / U+2265.

selectedClasses.description: Class values to match. Required for `class` operator only. Pixel matches if its band value is any one of these. Should reference values from the band's `legendEntries`.

from.description: Lower bound. Required for `range` operator only.

to.description: Upper bound. Required for `range` operator only.

fromInclusive.description: Whether the lower bound is inclusive. Required for `range` operator only.

toInclusive.description: Whether the upper bound is inclusive. Required for `range` operator only.

allOf.description: Range operator requires from, to, fromInclusive, toInclusive.
